name: Eleni Lefakis
statement: Hi! I’m Eleni Lefakis (she/her), and I’m a 3nd-year TAPS major. I’ve became involved with UT in first year and have worked on literally every show that would have me (and one or two that really had to be convinced to have me) since. I have worked on 12 mainstages and 6 workshops in the past three years and have served a little under two terms on committee (I initially joined via special election halfway through first year spring), the second of which I spent as Outreach Chair. I mostly focus on direction and management, although I recently realized that I hold way more dramaturgy credits than credits in any other department, which technically makes dramaturgy my primary focus. UT has been by far the most important thing in my life throughout college, and as someone who wants to go into arts administration after graduating, I’m really grateful for the community’s continued trust in me! This year on committee, we have continued to navigate UT in its post-pandemic, post-9 week quarter, post-quarterly proshow state, and I think we’ve adapted beautifully. We’ve spent much of this year putting out one fire after the other and it’s taught me a lot about how to behave in a crisis situation. Every issue we’ve come across this year has been solved thanks to our community’s commitment and flexibility, and I want committee to go into this year focusing on how we can best use those traits.  Personally, there are a few things I want to work on this year for UT. The first is outreach. I developed the idea of the Outreach Chair position last spring because I felt that committee’s communication with our members and audiences was lacking, but I think my efforts with our marketing this year are only part of the solution. There is still a lot left to do in expanding our recruitment practices and getting in contact with potential new members. Along with recruitment also comes training, which is the second thing I want to focus on. UT needs to work on training new members on both how to do their jobs and how the organization as a whole works. Organizational knowledge outside of committee level lacks, and I think next year is a great time to improve on that, as it is the first time since the pandemic that the majority of leadership and knowledge is concentrated in the graduating class of UT.  Committee’s work on the production manuals, bylaw re-writes, and new proposal process this year has set us up to do this next year and take the onus for training off of direct, face-to-face communication between people in the know and people who are looking to learn. I've worked really hard and learned so much in these past two terms, and I hope to have the opportunity to stay on committee and continue supporting the community in every way I can!

name: Will Rehmus
statement: Hi! I'm Will (they/them), and I'm a first-year. So far, I have been involved in 3 UT productions: Weekend of Workshops: Cassandra (Sound/Lighting Designer), Muscle Memory (Assistant Lighting Designer), and Falsettos (ASM), as well as participating in this quarter's Theater[24] festival. I am also currently working on Strings Attached (Co-Sound Designer) and Play That Goes Wrong (Assistant Lighting Designer). My main goal on committee would be to support the general functioning of UT and work with the committee and general membership to improve things where necessary. I am also particularly interested in theater accessibility: both making it easier to get involved and broadening the scope of people who can enjoy participating in and watching our performances.

name: Joseph DePaula
statement: Hi, I'm Joseph DePaula! I am not a TAPS major, but I am aware of its existence. I've been in the cast of 3 UT Mainstages (including Strings Attached right now), 1 Dean's Men production, and 1 Theater[24] over the last 2 years, and I also wrote for Theater[24] once this year! I'd like to be a part of committee because, in brief, I love theatre! And I love UT, and I want to take a bigger part in it & in the process of putting on the shows that UT does.

name: Belle Nahoom
statement: Hi! My name is Belle Nahoom and I'm a third year studying Data Science and Theatre and Performance Studies. I've worked on several productions in my last three years at the university and primarily consider myself a costume designer. During my last year in committee, I, along with several other members, focused on ensuring the accessibility of information/knowledge around working on UT productions. I want to continue working to establish general guidelines and understanding surrounding what UT does. In the next year, I hope to focus on fully developing assistantship relationships. By that I mean creating guidelines on how to best train assistants and ensure that we are working towards a UT community that expands rather than contracts. I believe that the best training helps retain new members and creates an inviting and exciting new environment for new and old members to interact. With the way University Theater is changing, with the annual calendar coming into effect, this is a great opportunity to accept these changes, learning about them ourselves while teaching newer members about how they can tackle the challenges of producing theater. I am so excited for the new year of theater! and I hope to work on shows with all of you in the future.

name: Coco Liu
statement: Hi! I’m Coco, and I'm the current Treasurer on UT. I’ve been heavily involved in UT throughout my time here, primarily as either stage or production management on a mainstage show almost every quarter. Besides being the treasurer, I’ve also helped to organize and run auditions, Staged Readings last year, and was involved in the revamp of the UT proposal process and show schedules last year. More recently, I'm leading the effort to create production manuals that serve as guides for every department (more on that coming very soon!!) During my time in UT, I’ve seen, heard, and experienced a lot about what’s working and what isn’t. Moving forward, if I’m given the opportunity to continue serving on Committee, my goal is to refine and improve our processes to reduce unnecessary stress and create experiences that are more enjoyable for everyone. As one of the Committee members who worked on the new season schedule implemented this past year, I recognize that some parts of it aren't working for us right now. I believe that we need to step back and take stock of what's working and what's not, and think about how we can improve our timelines to better support shows and teams. At the same time, I am also keen on improving and expanding our processes and mechanisms for training new members. This starts with the creation and dissemination of the production manuals, but that's just the beginning -- I also believe that more emphasis should be placed on training assistants, and UT needs to expand our repertoire of avenues for mentorship and advice. Ultimately, I hope to help to grow the UT community, retain current members, and enable everyone to have a fun, educational, and productive time! UT has been a massive part of my time at UChicago thus far, so I’m really invested in helping to build the community and improve our processes. Thanks for the consideration!

name: Kaden Kaden
statement: Hi!! I'm a first year here on campus and I have had so much fun participating in various UT endeavors for the last two quarters. Last quarter I started out by doing the lighting design for Theater [24], and I had a blast doing it again this quarter. Also last quarter, I joined the Muscle Memory team as Lighting Designer which was such an incredible experience. I'm so grateful for all I got to learn about putting on main-stage shows and for all the amazing people I got to meet! Most recently I was the costume designer for this quarter's Workshops and I loved getting to branch out from lighting and try something new. For upcoming/ongoing stuff: I'm lighting designer for The Play that Goes Wrong and costume assistant for Strings Attached; I can't wait to see how they turn out! I want to join the UT committee so I can be even more involved with the amazing stuff we get to do! When I first got to campus (which was not even that long ago, so honestly this all still applies) I was so grateful and amazed that I got to meet so many wonderful people through UT. I want to do my part to support the UT community and keep our organization moving forward towards new and exciting things. I hope I can be a part of the team that helps make UT what it is: a group of lovely people who love theater and want to have fun!

name: Abby Kanes
statement: Hello, my name is Abby Kanes, I am a third year TAPS and business economics major. Being an active member of University Theater since my first year, I have acted in multiple productions, played bass in pit orchestras as well as been on the crew. I have been a member of UT committee for the past two seasons and am excited to run again for this position. As a UT committee member, I have worked as Committee Liaison on productions as well as ran the UT instagram in the 2022-2023 season.  This past year has been very exciting for committee as we expanded to passing a whole season at once. However this change comes with a lot of challenges. These challenges were not obvious and last year we did a lot of work overcoming these obstacles, such as budget and booking spaces. As we plan for next year I would like to bring this knowledge from the past year into the new proposal cycle to make the whole year run much smoother.  One thing that has really improved but could still use more work is outreach for production staff as well as audience. A lot of work has been done in the last year on this and I believe we can only increase membership and audience through marketing and holding open events. Similarly, with the large number of productions each year it becomes increasingly difficult to staff these shows. For this new season to succeed we need to focus on recruitment and commitment to our organization and I am hoping to be a part of that this coming year. Thanks for your consideration!

name: Emily Curran
statement: Hi Everyone! My name is Emily Curran and I’m a second year majoring in Public Policy with a minor in TAPS. I have been on four UT shows and am currently in process with two more. On these shows, I worked as an assistant lighting designer, lead lighting designer, and an actor. I am also a curator for Theater [24] and have been a part of every show since my first year. I believe these experiences have given me a decent look into how UT functions on all sides.  Last year, I ran for committee to advocate for designers. This year, I am running for the same thing because I believe there is still work to be done. Strides have been taken to support designers such as design manuals. As someone who worked on the lighting manual, I think these are steps in the right direction. I think we can further this intent by making our best effort to have more experienced designers meet and work with newer folks either before they begin working on a show or in an assistant capacity. Creating these relationships could also serve to increase the amount of designers that are willing to stay on for multiple shows and years. In addition, I believe there can be improvements made to tech particularly with the aspect of time. UT has produced / is producing three musicals during my time here and I have been on the team either as a designer or actor for all three. Coming from a lighting designer’s perspective, the current amount of time given for tech makes the process extremely difficult. Especially if lead designers are to take on a kind of teaching role with their assistants as well. If elected, I would like to work towards a scheduling system that is not necessarily one size fits all. I believe this would lead to a more successful and enjoyable process.  UT has been a large part of my life at UChicago. I have been welcomed with open arms into the community and would like to do everything in my power to give back and make this process as fun as possible. Thank you for the opportunity to run!

name: Joshua Winston
statement: Hi Everyone! I'm Josh Winston, and I'm a 3rd year Near Eastern Languages and Civilizations Major. I've been acting in various UT shows since my freshman year, having been part of Trail to Oregon, Marian (or the True Tale of Robin Hood), The Laramie Project, Dead Fun Society, and currently The Play that Goes Wrong  If elected to Committee, I want to focus on making UT as welcoming and easy to adjust to for its new members as possible. Especially for people who have done theater before coming to UChicago, the transition to doing shows directed and run by your peers can be difficult to navigate. Specifically, many who join UT are not used to an environment where actors have set rights and can communicate directly with their director about anything that makes them uncomfortable. This is absolutely critical to our process and I wish to continue the work Committee and various directors have been doing to make sure new members are as aware of their rights as possible. This should also apply not only to actors, but to people who wish to join in production and design roles as well. Committee should ensure new designers are as aware of the resources available to them as possible and are comfortable voicing their concerns with their show's production manager and director.  We had a large influx of new people joining shows this year, something I hope to see happen again this spring and next year, and doing this will make UT as accessible and welcoming to them as possible. More than that, my hope is to continue the work Committee has been doing over the last two years to ensure that the rights of every member of UT are properly cemented. As is, actors have a variety of rights given to them by the Chicago Theater Standards, but designers especially have very little that is directly spelled out. My goal is to communicate openly with designers, both those on committee and those outside of it to ensure that this is completed as soon as possible. In my eyes, the most important step towards bettering everyone's experience with UT is that we break down the barrier between cast and crew that so often exists in shows. Everyone involved in a show should know everyone else involved in it, which has not always been the case in the past. It is my hope that bettering communication between actors and designers will fix problems that have come up in the past like actors not signing up for/not showing up for costume fittings or actors being blindsided tech week by design aspects that had been cemented long before that. Ideally, we can make shows feel more like a singular community instead of a split company and crew and foster a culture where actors are not left in the dark about design elements while ensuring designers receive the respect they deserve.

name: Lucienne Totti
statement: Although I might not have been the most involved in the past, I have a newfound love for UT. I am committed, hardworking, and love all things theater. Whether or not im in committee I will continue to put my best efforts into UT.

name: Ruth Witter
statement: Hey y'all! I've been an actor in two Dean's Men shows and will be working as Props Designer for the Play that Goes Wrong next quarter. I'm also trying to collect the trifecta of actor, director, and writer over three quarters of Theater[24] this year.  s a member of the UT committee I want to encourage greater collaboration between different groups, like the Dean's Men and Off-Off. I also want to look into doing more low-committment UT activities like Theater[24] in order to bring people into the theater community, even if they have other large commitments that would normally prevent them from participating.

name: Anna German
statement: Hello so excited to be here, I feel like Im always here. I have been costume designer and assistant on 6 shows and a workshop. I am very passionate about UT and I have been a part of a production every quarter I have been at UChicago. However, that means that I have been able to see many problems with the process. I know many people are frustrated with the way that UT works and I am convinced we can improve it. One thing I think that Committee is missing is input from designers, since almost none of the current members of Committee have been in a lead design position on a show, and there are a lot of concerns and considerations that can be hard to understand from the outside in. I would like there to be more representation for an underserved and underappreciated part of UT. I also think that there could be more input and consideration from members of UT who aren’t on Committee but still have great ideas for improvement. Furthermore, I would love to see more diversity within UT. I don’t think that we as an organization do enough right now to reach out to the larger UChicago community and, as a result, lose the chance to have a wider range of people involved in our productions. Overall, I am passionate about theater and I want it to succeed on this campus. While the work we do is great, I know we have the capacity for better.

name: Maya Carlos Doyle
statement: I’m Maya (she/her), a third-year in the College studying English. I have (technically) 14 UT/TAPS credits (as well as a season of curating T[24]) under my belt. Besides the handful of assistant and/or design roles I’ve done, I’ve primarily done management, and next quarter, I’ll be the PM on Midsummer. I was a general member of Committee last year, and I’d love to the opportunity to serve again! UT and TAPS have both gone through a lot of changes in the past year, from new Pro-Staff members to a restructured proposal cycle for UT shows (and the crop of first-years that come at the beginning of every year!!). I want to use my experience of previously serving on Committee, including passing UT’s first year-long season, and my experience on a range of productions to help guide UT forward.  The two things I’m interested for next year are building community and refining the proposal process. We’re putting on seven Mainstages this year, many of which overlap — not to mention Workshops and Staged Readings. This has led to some trouble staffing shows, on both the production and cast side. A more rigorous discussion on the way all the shows of the season would interact with each other is necessary in order to ensure our community and TAPS aren’t overloaded. Having been a part of the discussions last year on which shows to pass and now seeing the results of those decisions, I can better foresee possible issues with different proposals. This previous experience is invaluable as we continue to shape the next era of UT with this proposal schedule.  One positive is that with so many production roles come a lot of new designers, which we need as we say goodbye to our fourth-years. With them leaving, we lose valuable institutional knowledge. Community requires mentorship, and the future of UT depends on sustaining assistants as members of UT who will later want to SM,  design costumes, and so forth. We’ve discussed codifying assistant-ships, but not much has been done. I want to work more on making assistants feel welcome and emphasizing the role of a lead as a mentor.  If it wasn’t clear, I have loved and valued my time with UT, and I want to continue to give back to this RSO. Thanks for reading!

name: Giancarlo Beritela
statement: Hey UT, I’m Giancarlo Beritela, a second-year Econ and Romance Languages major, running for committee for the first time! Starting out as strictly an actor in Romeo & Juliet, Dead Fun Society, and Taming of the Shrew, this term I’m branching out, working as an assistant director for TPTGW, only having taken one quarter off during my first year. I hope to remain consistently involved and continue to branch out into new roles and experiences in UT! Despite having only been marginally interested in theater up until college, UT has stolen my heart and along with it much of my free time, also bleeding into my language coursework (as well as becoming a potential thesis topic, but I digress). As with most things I engage with, I try to not just appreciate them but strive to make them better, and I want to offer myself to UT in any way I can.  If elected to committee, I would best help improve UT’s accessibility and presence around campus and the university as a whole. As an RA on campus, I already play a particularly important role in helping new members of a community adjust, providing abundant opportunities to get involved, and passing down best practices, to say only a little. In a similar vein I would also work very hard to legitimately represent the voices of UT in its entirety, so that everyone may have the best experience possible. Thanks!

name: Christian Beltran
statement: Theater has changed my life. I've been doing it since freshman year of high school, and it completely transformed me as a person. I've gotten to know so many cool people, had so many amazing experiences, and helped maintain and heighten my high school's theater community. I joined the Thespian board at the end of my freshman year because I was interested in learning more about this captivating hobby, and by my senior year I became president because I wanted to do everything in my power to make sure others could have the same life-changing experience I had. When I got to UChicago, I immediately knew I wanted to continue doing theater. I was a little worried that college theater would be too hard and competitive, but everyone was very welcoming and I felt right at home. Now, having some experience with UT, I'd like to make sure it stays accessible to everyone. I'm also just very interested in learning more about theater and how it's done, and learning about all the little moving parts and behind-the-scene processes that most people don't get to see.
